sourceMovie.title: Léon: The Professional
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Léon: The Professional works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Colombiana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Léon: The Professional clicked for you, Colombiana (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Léon: The Professional?
faq.answer: Try Colombiana, Payback, The Enforcer—each shares DNA with Léon: The Professional while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Léon: The Professional?
faq.answer: Léon: The Professional (1994) is best described as Crime, Drama, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Léon: The Professional worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
